Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search Slack or emails for Marcus's flag about the Mosaic prototype insurance shortfall (query: "insurance gap" or "coverage gap" or "Mosaic damage"). Discover the $40K gap — vendor liability covers $50K per item but the prototype is valued at $90K.
Search emails for Emeka's incident report to Tamara Byrd (query: "incident report Tamara Byrd" or "Emeka Mosaic"). Discover that Emeka sent the report proposing replacement shipping at MoveOps' cost — an additional financial commitment that affects the accrual.
Search Slack #operations for additional damage claim context (query: "prototype damage" or "foam crating"). May discover Blessing Okafor didn't verify the foam density spec and the delivery receipt was signed by a Mosaic intern instead of Zara Okoye, complicating the claim window.
Search Slack #finance or emails for the DOT compliance extension status (query: "DOT extension" or "Linda Castellano"). Discover that Marcus's 15-day filing extension request was granted.
Search Slack #operations for which coordinator had the placarding certificate gap (query: "placarding certificate" or "documentation gap"). Discover it was Blessing Okafor who never filed the cert for the Mosaic laser cutter move (Keiko Tanaka).
Search emails for whether the signed placarding certificate was obtained from the carrier (query: "Greg Pallone" or "Swift placarding"). Discover that Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations confirmed the placarding was completed at time of transport (job #SR-2026-04-1538, driver Mike Becerra) and is having the signed originals pulled and scanned from Swift's compliance archive — potentially closing the documentation gap and reducing the fine exposure once received.
Search Linear for the DOT compliance tracking tickets Chloe created (query: "DOT" or "hazmat documentation" or "placarding").
Search Slack #finance or emails for the Heartland overbilling details (query: "Heartland overbilling" or "Heartland Q1 invoice" or "payment hold"). Discover that Heartland billed for 8 moves at $12,800 but only 5 were legitimate — Lily Marchetti and Danielle Osei were cancelled, Priya Venkatesh was handled by Swift. Total overbilling: $4,800. Corrected amount: $8,000.
Search Airtable Relocations for the authoritative move breakdown (query: "Heartland" or individual employee names like "Lily Marchetti", "Danielle Osei", "Priya Venkatesh") to verify which moves were completed, cancelled, or handled by a different vendor.
Search emails for a response from Jake Loomis at Heartland (query: "Jake Loomis" or "Heartland dispute response"). Discover that Chloe's cancellation emails were never acknowledged and check whether Marcus's formal dispute has received a reply.
Search Linear for the Heartland dispute tracking ticket Chloe created (query: "Heartland" or "overbilling" or "vendor dispute").
Look up the three contingent liability entries Hana originally booked in QuickBooks: the Mosaic damage accrual, the DOT fine accrual, and the $12,800 Heartland payment hold. These are the baseline figures to compare against current findings.
Look up Marcus Thorne's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne") to get his email address for the summary email.
Search emails for a recent message from Marcus Thorne indicating he is out of office (query: "out sick" or "OOO" or "out of office"). Discover that Marcus is out sick and has asked to be reached at his personal email trader_thorne@yahoo.com.
Update the QuickBooks accrual entries using quickbooks_update_account based on current findings (accruals are recorded as bills but quickbooks_update_account is used to adjust account-level balances since no bill update tool is available): adjust the Mosaic accrual for the $40K gap plus replacement shipping commitment, revise the DOT fine accrual downward since the cert was obtained and extension granted, and adjust the Heartland hold to reflect the $8,000 corrected amount vs $4,800 disputed.
Add status notes to the Linear tracking tickets for DOT and Heartland (and Mosaic if a ticket exists) using linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue, documenting the latest resolution status and any accrual adjustments made.
Send Marcus an email using send_email (to: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, cc: trader_thorne@yahoo.com) summarizing the revised exposure numbers for all three items — Mosaic damage ($40K gap + shipping commitment), DOT compliance (extension granted, cert obtained, accrual adjusted), and Heartland (5/8 legitimate, $4,800 disputed, Jake Loomis response status) — so he has the updated figures for Elena. CC his personal email since he is out sick.
Post the updated contingent liability figures in #finance using conversations_add_message so Alejandro Fuentes can double-check the numbers before Marcus's briefing with Elena.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 At least one QuickBooks accrual or account entry was updated using a QuickBooks write tool.
outcome

—Run #2No justification
